title: Logout undone by in-flight session refresh (stale async write)
product_summary: Acme Portal - vanilla-JS customer portal SPA with a module-level session store

ground_truth.root_cause: logout() in src/state/session.js clears the module-level store synchronously, but refreshUser() applies its fetched user unconditionally after its await; the portal auto-refreshes the session every 60s (src/ui/app.js), so a refresh in flight when the user clicks log out resolves afterwards and re-populates state.user, making the account reappear. Stale async writes need to be discarded after logout (session epoch/generation counter or request abort).
ground_truth.fix_contract: A refreshUser() call that resolves after logout() must not restore the user: after logout the store stays signed out (getUser() null, isAuthenticated() false) even when the in-flight refresh settles. The normal lifecycle keeps working: login signs in, an uninterrupted refresh applies updated user data, and login plus refresh after a logout behave normally.
expected_evidence: the user_logged_out -> session_refreshed funnel showing most logouts are followed by a session_refreshed event in the same session
session replays / tickets describing the header flipping to signed-out and the account reappearing moments later
distractors: cookie or local-storage caching blamed by customers (the auth token is cleared correctly on logout)
src/api/client.js request layer (returns correct data; the staleness bug is in how session state applies it)
